title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement with the Providence Bruins, ending his collegiate career at Boston College.
Hagens, the 7th pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, joins Providence Bruins practice at Amica Mutual Pavilion on Tuesday.
Hagens is expected to play his first game against the Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday night.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer aims to shore up Red Sox's infield, but durability concerns remain with his past injury-ridden seasons.
Roman Anthony is viewed as a potential MVP candidate, leading the Red Sox lineup in his first full MLB season.
Payton Tolle, a top Red Sox prospect, may be called up as bullpen reinforcement if injuries affect the team.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Rep. Bradley Jones announces he won't seek reelection after 32 years, ending his tenure in January 2027.
Jones has served the 20th Middlesex District and has been Republican Minority Leader since 2002.
Amy Carnevale applauds Jones' dedication and impact on Massachusetts' Republican Party and his constituents.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: ICE agents not deployed to New England airports, including Logan, as federal agents are sent to major airports.
DHS officers conducted a 'Visible Intermodal Prevention and Response' operation at Logan Airport in Boston.
ICE agents deployed to 14 airports, while Logan Airport avoided significant delays faced by other major hubs.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, confirmed by Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty has been appointed as acting chief following Suarez's leave starting February 26.
Suarez, appointed chief in 2023, previously served as provisional chief since 2021 under former Mayor Stephen DiNatale.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing incident on Seaport Boulevard, suffering wounds to his abdomen and leg.
The incident occurred around 2:44 a.m. Sunday outside 72 Seaport Blvd; the victim was found near Kings Dining and Entertainment.
Police have not identified any suspects and continue to investigate the stabbing with no arrests made so far.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial began in Essex County Superior Court, focusing on her assault and battery charges.
Fitzsimmons, a police officer, was shot by colleague Patrick Noonan after allegedly aiming a weapon at him.
Prosecutors and defense offered differing narratives on whether Fitzsimmons intended self-harm or threatened officers.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena after their Rhode Island state championship win.
Blackstone Valley achieved their first state title after a dramatic quadruple-overtime victory against Lincoln High School.
The visit was emotional as club members honored Colin Dorgan, who led the team after a personal tragedy in February.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots have signed offensive tackle James Hudson, who previously played for the Giants and Browns.
Hudson has appeared in 60 games with 19 starts, primarily at right tackle, during his five-year NFL career.
The Patriots signed Hudson to add depth at tackle, backing up Morgan Moses and potentially covering for Will Campbell.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ends his sophomore season at Boston College and is yet to sign with the Boston Bruins.
The Bruins may place Hagens with the Providence Bruins or directly in the NHL depending on contract talks.
Coach Marco Sturm is open to adding young prospects like Hagens into the Bruins lineup if they are ready.
